title: Post-Secondary Education Account (PSEA)
text:
Post-Secondary Education Account (PSEA) - SupportGoWhere
Post-Secondary Education Account (PSEA)
Share link
Provides educational financial support for eligible Singapore Citizens' post-secondary education.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Funds can be used for approved programmes, repay government loans, and financial schemes
No application needed
Collapse all sections
About this support

PSEA Funds can be used to pay:

one's own or siblings’ approved education programmes at approved institutions

government education loans and financial schemes

PSEA balances earn interest at the same rate as the CPF Ordinary Account (CPF-OA), currently 2.5% per annum

Unused PSEA funds will be transferred to the CPF-OA when the account holder turns 31, subject to CPF-OA usage rules

Who is this for?

You must be a Singapore Citizen with a balance in either:

the Child Development Account (CDA) when turning 13 years old

the Edusave Account when turning 17 years old or is no longer studying in a Ministry of Education (MOE)-funded school, whichever is later

The PSEA will also be opened for Singapore Citizens who are:

eligible for PSEA top-ups by the Government

recipient of the first payment of the National Service Housing, Medical and Education (NS HOME) Awards

recipient of the annual education bursary under the Home Ownership Plus Education (HOPE) Scheme

How to apply?

No application required.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MOE Hotline:6872 2220

Submit an enquiry

MOE Customer Service Centre, 1 North Buona Vista Drive MOE Building, Podium Block Singapore 138675

Note: By appointment only.

Scheme last updated 02 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
